problem: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism?
mechanism: Fear memory reconsolidation interference via timing
hypothesis: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Fear. Fear memory reconsolidation interference via timing directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: EEG/MEG signal analysis with 7 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: To know that you do not know is the best. — Lao Tzu
